item.title: Why I Kept A Music Page
item.description:
I did not want the music part of the site to be an afterthought hidden in bookmarks or playlist screenshots.

A small dedicated page is enough:

  • it gives me a place to keep a hand-picked list
  • it lets me add context when a song matters for a specific reason
  • it keeps the site personal instead of purely professional

item.title: Notes Worth Keeping
item.description:
Not everything needs to be a full technical article.

Some ideas are better captured as short notes: things I noticed, decisions I made, links I want to remember, or small observations from the day that are still worth writing down.

This section is for that kind of writing.

item.title: What Will Show Up Here
item.description:
The technical side of this site is for posts that deserve more room than a thread or a README update.

Expect things like:

  • implementation notes after building or debugging something
  • architecture tradeoffs that are easier to explain with examples
  • practical write-ups that start from a real problem and end with a working fix

If a post saves me from relearning something six months later, it belongs here.

item.title: Start Here
item.description:
This site has three lanes:

  • technical write-ups when I want to explain something properly
  • shorter notes on day-to-day affairs when an idea is still fresh
  • a music page for songs that survive repeated listening

The homepage stays simple on purpose. This pinned post acts as the map, and the rest of the page stays reserved for the latest writing.

What To Expect

The technical posts will be slower and more detailed.

item.title: Music
item.description:
This is where I keep a short public list of songs and records worth replaying.

Current Rotation

  • Replace this line with a song title and artist.
  • Replace this line with another track you keep coming back to.
  • Add a live version, remix, or album cut when it matters.

Why Keep A Music Page

Good songs disappear into playlists too easily. A small hand-picked list is a better record of taste than an endlessly growing library.

item.title: Updates
item.description:
This page is for quick status notes about the site and what I am actively working on.

March 2026

  • Laid out the site around technical writing, short-form notes, and music.
  • Cleaned out the starter content and turned the homepage into a live feed of the latest posts.
  • Added the basic SEO plumbing: sitemap, robots rules, favicon assets, and Google Analytics configuration hooks.

What Comes Next

  • Publish the first real technical deep dive.
  • Keep this page current with what is new instead of treating the homepage like a changelog.

item.title: Writing
item.description:
This section holds the longer pieces.

The split is simple:

  • Technical write-ups for software, systems, and implementation details.
  • Day-to-day notes for shorter reflections and public thinking.

The homepage shows the newest posts first. The archive shows everything in one place when the list gets longer.

item.title: About
item.description:
This site is the long-form version of my notes.

I use it for three things:

  • Technical write-ups that are too detailed for social posts.
  • Shorter notes on day-to-day affairs, ideas, and things worth keeping.
  • A music page for songs that stay in rotation longer than a week.

What You Will Find Here

Technical writing

Architecture notes, debugging stories, implementation details, and practical lessons from building software.

Day-to-day notes
